title: 1979 Bank Nationalization & Consolidation Event Log
title_fa: گاه‌شمار ملی‌شدن و ادغام بانک‌ها در 1979
description: Event log (nationalization decree, 5-bank consolidation into Ma'dan-va-San'at/Maskan/Keshavarzi/Tejarat/Mellat, which banks stayed unmerged, the sole non-nationalized bank Bank Iran-o-Rus, the 1983-84 Islamic-banking law) -- not a numeric time series, no existing chart_id concept applies.

citations.source_org: Compiled by this project from multiple secondary/academic sources (curated, hand-compiled -- see docs/bookkeeping.md rule 5)

Restored from the audit log's documented fix + the cited manifest.json files.

title: Post-Privatization Private Bank Founding/Licensing Timeline (2000-2025)
title_fa: روند تأسیس و صدور مجوز بانک‌های خصوصی پس از خصوصی‌سازی (2000-2025)
description: Entity-level founding/licensing-date log for Iran's post-2000 private banking wave, ending with Ayandeh Bank's 2025 CBI-ordered dissolution/merger into Bank Melli -- not a numeric time series, no existing chart_id concept applies (WDI's banking-access indicators are aggregate ratios, not named-entity timelines).

title: Iran/Iraq Trade & Oil-Export-Revenue Citations (1987)
title_fa: استنادات تجارت و درآمد صادرات نفت ایران/عراق (1987)
description: Iraq's $33bn cumulative Arab war aid ($27bn from Saudi/Kuwait alone), Baghdad's OPEC-quota-parity dispute with Iran, plus detailed bilateral trade figures.

citations.source_org: Central Intelligence Agency (CIA), Directorate of Intelligence, Office of Near Eastern and South Asian Analysis (NESA)

title: Iran-Iraq Economic Balance Under the 1986 Oil-Price Collapse
title_fa: توازن اقتصادی ایران و عراق در پی سقوط قیمت نفت در 1986
description: Iran-Iraq Economic Balance Under the 1986 Oil-Price Collapse

title: Pre-Revolution Shah's Economy Overview per CIA (1974)
title_fa: نمای کلی اقتصاد ایران در دوران شاه پیش از انقلاب به روایت CIA (1974)
description: 33%/40% GNP growth (1973/1974 expected), Fifth Plan spending expansion $23bn planned -> $45bn actual, ~$20bn/yr oil-revenue outlook, $8bn 1974 lending/investment commitments, $8bn cumulative 1967-73 arms spending, inflation acceleration 6% -> 11% -> 16% (1972-Q1 1974), top/bottom-20%-income-share inequality.

citations.source_org: Central Intelligence Agency (CIA), Office of Economic Research (OER)

title: Iran vs. Iraq Wartime Demographic, Health & Macro Comparison
title_fa: مقایسه جمعیتی، سلامت و کلان اقتصادی ایران و عراق در دوران جنگ
description: Part of the full Table 1 side-by-side Iran/Iraq country comparison plus Tehran-ghetto housing statistics (two-thirds of families in single-room housing, 6,000 fires/year).

citations.source_org: Central Intelligence Agency (CIA), Directorate of Intelligence, Office of Near Eastern and South Asian Analysis (NESA)

title: Iran vs. Iraq Wartime External-Sector Comparison: Debt, FX, Trade & Oil
title_fa: مقایسه بخش خارجی ایران و عراق در دوران جنگ: بدهی، ارز، تجارت و نفت
description: Iraq's $40bn non-Arab debt kept explicitly separate from $35bn Gulf-Arab soft debt per the source's own framing (never summed); Iran's oil-production-capacity halving (~6m -> ~3m b/d, 1978-88); the US-Iran trade collapse ($4bn/1978 -> $60m/1987 exports).

citations.source_org: Central Intelligence Agency (CIA), Directorate of Intelligence, Office of Near Eastern and South Asian Analysis (NESA)

title: Iran vs. Iraq Wartime Military, Infrastructure & Government-Finance Citations

citations.source_org: Central Intelligence Agency (CIA), Directorate of Intelligence, Office of Near Eastern and South Asian Analysis (NESA)
